transcript.text: Can you hear me okay?
You're on mute.
You can't hear me because I'm muted.
Yeah.
They changed that setting.
meeting is being recorded.
I have never gotten used to it.
I don't know what I'm doing.
It's like I keep forgetting to do it.
How you been?
Yeah, I'm all right.
How about yourself?
It's like we're getting the band back together.
Yeah, that's right.
Jen messaged me the other day and she's like, are you back on discern?
And I said, yeah, I sure am.
And she's like, oh, good.
So she said to tell you hi.
Okay.
Hello.
Okay.
So we're back on track with production.
I know we had a little snag there with some technical issues where we were waiting on, I think, calibration and then they had to get things set up for us.
But I think we're on track now.
I was trying to get an update before the meeting, but I haven't heard back yet.
But it looks like we've got like 40, I think, roughly, articles that are ready to be staged and scheduled for publishing.
So we're
Um, that's pretty good there.
And then did you, the last one you gave feedback on was the real estate article, correct?
Or have you, have you seen that we have a revised one?
I think that's what.
Well, do you want to send me the article?
I'll tell you.
Yeah, that's what I was checking.
I didn't know if you'd gotten it or not.
I couldn't remember if I could send it or not.
Let me, let me do that.
And I will make sure that you get it.
So I got to find it first.
So I know it's there.
Okay.
I will do that for you immediately after the meeting.
Um, and then, um, for next week, um, we just wanted to do like a quarterly business review with you.
Um, kind of make sure that where you've got the right priorities start, especially going into 2026 and obviously with me kind of coming back on the account.
Um, so we need like about an hour next week, but we can do that in lieu of our regular sync.
So we're not taking up too much of your time next week.
So, um, if you want to float me like a couple times that might be good.
I don't know if we need to loop anybody else in or if you just want to just want it to be you on our end.
I'll probably probably Andy will probably join us.
But yeah.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I can send you next week.
I'm traveling for a lot of the week.
So I'll to figure that out.
You can do it the following if that works better.
It doesn't it's probably yeah, I don't want to make it stressful.
Yeah, probably does work better to do it the week after.
Sure.
Okay.
I will make a note of that.
But yeah, just float me a couple days that'll work for you when you get back and we'll take care of it.
We'll get it scheduled.
Performance wise, man, just January continues to be really, really strong.
It's just just this upward trajectory is just amazing to see.
So I'm glad that we were able to like continue to grow after after I handed it off.
It's nice to come back and things that things haven't fallen.
I didn't think they would, but it's nice to come back to like, oh, it's still in good shape.
And we're still seeing.
An increase in non-branded impressions.

We did see a little bit of performance falling across these cohorts, but because it's such a small amount, I think one little blip in the month is not, I don't think anything to like worry about week to week since it's doing so well overall.
We didn't have any major changes in the Scrunch data, but I'm going to show you our new AI visibility platform that we're going to be using going forward, which will replace how we report for you.
We'll replace Scrunch for that, and I'm going to demo that for you today.
But you're still getting quite a bit of referral traffic from LLMs, which is really positive.
And I think when I show you this in our tool, you'll see that because of the way that we categorize and how we track prompts, that actually discerns got more visibility than the competition.
any questions on that, though, before we go?
No, that makes sense.
Okay, cool.
Let's do this little demo.
So.
So our, so this is the internal AI visibility tool that GrowthX is developing called Check That.
It's on the surface, obviously, this looks a lot like the scrunch data, right?
There's not a lot of differences here from that chart I was just showing you.
It has the same charts.
It has the same rankings, uses the same position, sentiment, and citation share.

Where the difference really comes in is the idea behind this, Marcel's idea was to like create kind of like what G2 has done for reviews and like Crunchbase has done for funding, like kind of create this, but for AI visibility.
And one of the things that's really crucial to how we track this is that we focused almost exclusively on evaluation stage prompts.
There's about 100,000 across like something like 1,500 different categories and subcategories and whatnot.
And we're focused on that buying stage.
So there's a lot more like valuable information as opposed to people just searching about a topic related to your product.
They're actually searching for...
Solutions that, you know, discern does or search searching for discern specifically to compare it to other things.
So we're focused heavily in that area.
Doesn't mean you can't track other things, but that's where our library of prompts is.
And what's nice about the library of prompts is that you from day one have all this data.
So like if we were to add a new prompt from our shared library, like you would have whatever data we already have.
You're not having to wait like we did with Scrunch for it to like build out some to show you some numbers.
And so I can show you kind of what this looks like in terms of different prompts.
We already ported over or should have, if you see anything that's not looking right, the prompts from Scrunch.
And so you get kind of a similar breakdown here as you would in Scrunch for each prompt.
And when you click through, you also get to see by platform, like which ones are citing and mentioning discern.
And then clicking through them, you can see the actual response.
So like this one's probably a good one to look at.
So it's, you know, the question which platform automates both franchise taxes and annual reports.
It mentions First Base, Discern, and Sage, Intact.
What's nice that I love about this is that it actually shows you what page it's actually citing.
So you can see, like this is, I think, just going to your homepage.
But if this were going to a blog, we'd be able to see that specific traffic, like what that's coming from that, or potentially can come from that.
And then it also shows you the full response.
And it's across all of these, right?
So we can see, like, even like in Google AI overviews, you can see that that was the response given when somebody Googled, you know, platform automating this, right?
And in this instance, you're twice.
One.
So automating annual reports.
And the other one is Dell alternatives, which is nice.
Yeah, yeah, And so we can, but we can bring this back, like, you know, we can do 45 days of specific responses.
You can see how this gets more and more volatile.
further back you go with it, right?
Up, down, up, down.
That's pretty universal across accounts.
And you can see also, you know, that you're, like, 78% of the, you know, brands mentioned is going to discern, which is nice, and a lot of the citations as well.
So this is one of the better, honestly, the better breakdowns I've seen across clients, which is cool.
And I want to make sure I can cover everything.
This is still kind of rough around the edges, too.
So if we see glitches, let us know, because we can, they're probably bugs are still, we're still in a beta with this, but that's why we're using it internally for.
All GrowthX clients.
Going forward, this will be part of our offerings for our current clients.
And since you're up for renewal, you'd be getting a year of access for yourself, too, so that you can actually see how it works and look at it across the board.
But it is part of the package, like, you know, what we would charge for going forward.
But even if you stay a client and don't have your own access, we still use it for reporting.
So you're still getting the benefit of it either way.
Part of what makes this a lot more accurate, too, than Scrunch is, first of all, the context that we're using.
So we didn't just go, like, and search brand names.
We also searched with – it searches based on context.
So it's using that exact brand.
You know, we're not going to just search for any, like, Sage product, right?
It's going to be – it's going to be comparative against, like, what you're – what the prompt is asking.

And then I like this page, too, and I think this is going to, I think, quickly become one of my favorite things to, like, check, which is apparently, we're probably, probably have a lot of people demoing this today.
I know we've had a lot of updates in the last 24 hours, too.
But the sources page gives you a list of, like, the top links that are being cited.
And here we go.
And what I like about this is it kind of operates a little bit like a SERP would, but for AI visibility.
So these are pages that are consistently being cited by the models, both in, you know, your category, your competitors.
This is pulling from all that data we've given it.
And you can see, like, okay, 10 top business intelligence tools.
Is that something that we would want to create content for you for?
Maybe, maybe not.
And we can kind of look at this to see, like, what's getting attention.
And ideally, you know, there would be some discern pages coming up here.
Here, but I don't see any here yet.
There's one.
There's one.
I just saw it.
I caught the owl out of the corner of my eye, the logo.
Yeah.
So this is really, really helpful.
And I really kind of like even 1% is really strong because like this 5%, I've seen it top out at 6%.
I've never seen one get more than 6% of the citations.
And then the citation count is usually around 150.
So that's still pretty solid, even not far down the list having that.
So really good way, too, to get visibility into what kind of content is getting cited.
You'll notice the commonality there of all those like best of lists.
So that's one of the reasons we're doing that.
And that's coming from actual context, which we haven't even filled out yet for discern.
So that'll get more specific, too.
We can pull this from the artifacts that we're currently using to generate content.
But this is also part of like how it affects like how you're categorized and like what sort of prompts are recommended.
and
And then, of course, the competitors we have full control over.
I think I'm missing two that we had in Scrunch, but I pulled in the ones that I knew of, but we can continue to add those.
And you can see, too, you can also get to these, to like, you can go see their public facing page and like what information is available.
And this is really neat, too.
Even if you weren't, you you didn't have an account, this is, you know, basically their AI visibility, what's publicly facing.
We make a lot of our data actually publicly available, so there's not a lot of, it's not like a total black box, like some of the other tools are going into it.
And it shows you some of the latest, like, mentions.
So, also, this is a good resource for saying, oh, maybe we want to go after one of those if we're not already in the mentions for that.
Any other, any questions?
I feel like I've gone through a lot very quickly.
No, it makes sense.
Cool tool.
Yeah, yeah, we got a lot of, um...
So we've got a lot of really cool features planned for it, too.

Like right now, the initial features like this are all built out, but we're continuing to add things like, you know, a lot of, I don't know if you've seen some of the stuff like G2 does, but like they'll do like a page for a category and they'll kind of editorialize a little bit and like, here's the best one for this use case and here's the best platform for this.

We're doing something similar to that and then also like if you saw one, you're like, I want to track this prompt, too, that you can kind of, so people can find prompts in a variety of ways, not just what you're wanting to do.
Another thing is, like I said, we have that library of prompts.
These right here are all custom, but if I filter this through to the library, these ones that have this logo here, these are ones that are maintained by our editorial team.
So we're monitoring these all the time, but they tend to be more generic.
So you see like when I switch this view, they're more broad.
Because they're based on category, not on specifically what you want to track.
So sometimes these are good because they give you really good ideas.

But in your case, like I think that the custom ones are giving us a better insight into specifically the prompts that you want to be showing up for, which is why we're seeing such higher numbers there.
So anyway, but we have the ability to add from any category too.
Cool.
I think that's it, man.
I think we're good.
We're strong.
Unless you have anything you want me to cover, I'll send you that real estate article.
Make sure that you have the right, the current version.
Is there anything else that we need to go over?
No, we're good.
The silence worried me temporarily.
was like, did I miss something?
was just thinking.
Yeah, no, we're good.
Just tag me in anything that you need me to do.
I will.
will.
Absolutely.
I'm looking forward
I'm to the QBR next week, or two weeks from now, I'll have our team schedule something if you give me just a couple of things.
All right, and I'll follow up after the meeting with what we talked about.
Sounds good, Carrie.
All right.
Good to see you again.
Yeah, no problem.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync to review production, performance, and introduce a new reporting tool.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=37.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Production is back on track** after technical delays (calibration, setup), with \~40 articles now ready for staging and scheduling.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=37.0)
  - [**January performance is very strong,** with a continued upward trend in non-branded impressions and high referral traffic from LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [**GrowthX is replacing Scrunch with "Check That,"** a new AI visibility platform that tracks high-intent, evaluation-stage prompts to provide more valuable data.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=197.0)
  - [**A 1-hour Quarterly Business Review (QBR)** is scheduled for the week of Feb 9 to align 2026 priorities; it will replace the regular sync.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)

## Topics

### Production & Content

  - [Production is back on track after technical delays with calibration and setup.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=37.0)
  - [\~40 articles are ready for staging and scheduling.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=48.0)
  - [Mike Bosserman needs to review the revised real estate article.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=62.0)

### Performance & AI Visibility

  - [January performance is very strong, continuing a positive upward trend.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [Non-branded impressions are increasing.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [Referral traffic from LLMs remains high.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
  - [A minor performance dip in small cohorts is not a concern given strong overall growth.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=182.0)

### Introducing "Check That" Platform

  - [GrowthX is replacing Scrunch with "Check That," its new internal AI visibility platform.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=197.0)
  - [**Core Strategy:** Focuses exclusively on high-intent, evaluation-stage prompts (e.g., comparisons, alternatives) to provide more valuable data than general topic searches.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)
  - [**Key Features:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [**Instant Data:** Access to a 100k+ prompt library provides historical data from day one, eliminating Scrunch's data-collection lag.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [**Contextual Tracking:** Searches are based on context, not just brand names, ensuring accurate comparisons (e.g., "Sage Intacct" vs. "Sage").](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=512.0)
      - [**Source Tracking:** Shows the exact page cited by an LLM, enabling direct traffic attribution and content optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)
      - [**"Sources" Page:** Functions like a SERP for AI, listing top-cited pages (often "best of" lists) to identify content opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [**Discern's Performance in "Check That":**](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
      - [High visibility: 78% of brand mentions in tracked prompts.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
      - [Strong citation share: One article has a 1% share, which is significant as the platform's observed maximum is \~6%.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=597.0)
  - [**Access & Pricing:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0)
      - [GrowthX will use "Check That" for all client reporting.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0)
      - [Discern will receive a year of direct platform access upon renewal.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Carrie Chowske:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
      - [Send the revised real estate article to Mike Bosserman.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=62.0)
      - [Schedule the 1-hour QBR for the week of Feb 9.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)
  - [**Mike Bosserman:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=62.0)
      - [Review the real estate article.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=62.0)
      - [Provide availability for the QBR.](https://fathom.video/share/Nor3mQ5F-QsxTbu8uoA_Q7xQmXPDtbE4?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)

action_items.description: Email Mike revised real estate article
Email Carrie 2–3 QBR slots for week after next; then GrowthX schedules 1h QBR
